title: Synthesis: ideas.md through the Epistemic Engine Lens
description: Re-evaluating the propositions in ideas.md using the newly formalized principles (Storage != Projection, Map != Territory, Browser as VR Goggles). Proposition 1 ('Markdown is a tree... build graph databases') represents a fundamental category error if applied literally: Markdown is a declarative representation (the map), not the underlying relational truth (the territory). The graph database must be built in the underlying state ledger (the JSON events/nodes) and then projected into Markdown for human viewing. Proposition 2 regarding 'prompts', 'agents', and 'skills': A prompt is a declarative specification of intent given to an LLM rendering engine, much like HTML is to a browser. Agents and skills are the imperative execution functions (or declarative configurations) that operate on the graph. While humans may edit them via a Markdown representation (e.g., SKILL.md), they exist conceptually as capability nodes within the Engine's truth layer.
